sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
You can mix different guns together to counter of the weaknesses of the other guns, or just stick with one gun and have 6 of them.
Weapon

Notes

 Pistol

High powered slow guns that deal heavy damage, but can struggle dealing with bigger crowds of enemies.

 Laser Gun

 Revolver

 SMG

Very fast attack speed and can achieve great damage per second when scaled with ranged damage.

 Shredder

Can explode hordes of enemies in seconds, but struggles with dealing single target damage.

 Crossbow

High damage weapon like Pistol, but scales better with Range and Critical damage, rather than Ranged Damage.

sections.heading: Character
sections.text: Mage is a playable character in the game. Individual characters differ in their Stats and traits, to encourage a variety of play styles in Brotato.

sections.heading: How to Unlock
sections.text: Unlocked by default

sections.heading: Unlocks
sections.text:
Unlocks Thunder Sword if you win a game with Mage:
Thunder Sword

sections.heading: Starting Weapons
sections.text:
Mage has a selection from lower tier Elemental weapons and two guns. Wand and Torch inflict powerful burn on hit, while Taser and lightning shiv can create multiple projectiles that scale with elemental damage.
It is worth noting that any weapon that only scales with either Melee Damage or Ranged Damage can't be increased past their base damage. This includes SMG, and Double Barrel Shotgun. However the fast attack speed of the SMG and the piercing/multiple projectiles of the Double Barrel Shotgun can be used to spread burn with Scared Sausage. The projectiles of the Taser and the melee attack part of the lightning shiv can also spread fire in this way. (The projectiles created by the lightning shiv can inflict burn. This used to not be the case, but was changed in patch 1.0.1.3.).
Wand
 Torch
 Plank
 Taser
 Lightning Shiv
 Icicle
 SMG
 Double Barrel Shotgun

sections.heading: Starting Items
sections.text:
Mage starts with 2 items that make them more effective with the Elemental Damage.
Item

Effect

Notes

Scared Sausage

Attacks have a 25% chance to deal 3x1 (+100%) burning damage

Lets Mage use any weapon to inflict burn.
Increases the burn damage of weapons that already inflict burn by +1 (eg. Wand and Torch) 
But it is cheaper to buy more elemental damage if using those weapons. 
The 2nd, 3rd, 4th Scared Sausage also increases the damage done by the burn it inflicts by +1, but this isn't shown in the tooltip.

Snake

Burning spreads to an additional nearby enemy-1 Max HP

Snakes help spread your flames to more targets. You spread the fire to +1 additional enemy for each snake you have.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text:
1.1.0.0: Elemental Damage modifications: +33% => +25%. Engineering modifications: -100% => -50%

sections.text:
Chunky has a wide selection of weapons both Melee and Ranged.
Fist
 Cacti Club
 Rock
 Stick
 Torch
 Plank
 Chopper
 Pruner
 Spiky Shield
 Double Barrel Shotgun
 
 
 Taser

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Chunky has synergies with several of their starting weapons.
Weapon

Notes

 Hand

Unarmed class - Gives Dodge, which helps you avoid damage (but note that Chunky has -50% Dodge modifications)

 Fist

 Rock

Primitive class - Increases your Max HP.
Blunt class - Chunky ignores its speed reduction penalty.

 Cacti Club

Primitive class - Increases your Max HP.

 Stick

 Torch

 Plank

No remarkable synergy/effect

 Chopper

Increases healing with consumables, and scales in damage with your Max HP.

 Double Barrel Shotgun

Ranged weapon - Helps you avoid taking damage by attacking from a distance.

 Shredder

 Ghost Scepter

Ranged weapon - Helps you avoid taking damage by attacking from a distance.
Gives Max HP when killing enemies.

 Spiky Shield

Blunt class - Chunky ignores its speed reduction penalty. Spiky Shield also scales with armor, a thing that Chunky often stacks.

sections.heading: Healing
sections.text:
Chunky's low HP Regeneration scaling and irreparably low Life Steal make it difficult to reliably recover HP. Low Dodge scaling also means that the most effective way to improve survivability is to invest heavily into Max HP and Armor to simply be able to take as many hits as possible. HP Regeneration and Dodge can still be beneficial, but they will require twice the investment for the same value compared to other characters.
The one efficient source of HP recovery for Chunky is Luck. Luck increases the chance for Enemies to drop consumables which recover a base of 3 HP.
Chunky heals an additional +3 HP from consumables for a total of 6, and further investment in "+X HP recovered from consumables" through Lemonade and Weird Food is easily manageable.
The Tree item can also provide more consumables from trees as well as more item drops when combined with a high Luck stat.
Other sources of supplemental HP recovery include:
Cute Monkey - Heals an average of 8 HP per 100 Materials collected. Stacking multiple Cute Monkeys will provide considerable healing.
 Medical Turret - Heals 3 HP every 3 seconds while within the turret's range.

sections.heading: Padding
sections.text: Chunky's ability only grants Damage for permanent increases to your max HP. Max HP Increases granted by Padding's ability does not increase your Damage.

sections.heading: Speed
sections.text:
Since Speed modifications are reduced by 100%, Chunky's movement speed cannot be modified from its base value. All Speed increases and decreases from items should be disregarded when considering whether or not to purchase them. Esty's Couch, for example, can never provide Chunky with HP Regeneration since Speed cannot be reduced below 100%, effectively turning it into nothing more than a very expensive increase of 6.25 Max HP and ~2% Damage.
However the items with Speed Decreases become very attractive options.

sections.heading: Synergies
sections.text:
Here is a list of Items that have synergy with Chunky, such as negative speed, or that gives a good amount of Max HP.
Item

Effect

Notes

Helmet

+1 Armor-2 % Speed

Helmet increases your armor, an important stat for Chunky. You get to ignore the speed downside.

Warrior Helmet

+3 Armor+5 Max HP-5 % Speed

Warrior Helmet increases your armor and Max HP, two important stats for Chunky. You get to ignore the speed downside.

Lemonade

+1 HP recovered from consumables

Lemonade increases the amount of healing gained from consumables, one of Chunky's only way to reliably regain HP.

Weird Food

+2 HP recovered from consumables-2 % Dodge

Weird Food increases the amount of healing gained from consumables, one of Chunky's only way to reliably regain HP. The Dodge downside is halved, or can be fully ignored if you don't use dodge.

Torture

+15 Max HPRestore 5 HP per second. Cannot heal any other way.

Torture Provides a large amount of Max HP and a fixed amount of healing of 4 HP each second. Can be powerful if you didn't pick up other means of healing. But be careful, it prevents you from healing with consumables, so it might not be worth it in a lot of cases.

Snail

-8 % Enemy Speed-3 % Speed

Snail slows down the enemies. If you can't increase your own speed, you can just decrease the speed of the enemies. You also get to ignore the downside of -3% speed.

Ugly Tooth

Hitting an enemy removes 5% of their speed. Max 20%-3 % Speed

Ugly Tooth slows down the enemies on hit. Another way to compensate for your lack of speed. You also get to ignore the downside of -3% speed.

sections.heading: Pacifist
sections.text:
The Pacifist starts with a large negative to Damage and Engineering, preventing them from dealing damage to enemies.
Not killing enemies is normally a big problem, since it means you don't get Materials. That isn't a problem for the Pacifist. You get an amount of XP and Gold equal to 65% of the enemies on the screen, and 65% of the enemies that despawned during the wave. There is a limit of 100 enemies that can be on the screen at once. If more spawns, a random enemy is despawned without dropping loot. Pacifist is the exception when it comes to being rewarded for these. The limit excludes Elites, Bosses, and Trees.
However, the Pacifist only gains 1 Material for each enemy, even though that enemy might have dropped several materials upon death. But on the other hand, once you reach wave 5+ enemies' drop chance for materials begin to decrease. See the Materials Page.
On Horde Waves the amount of materials gained is halved. But the vast amount of extra creatures help make up for it.
While Pacifist might get slightly less materials from their pacifist mechanic compared to just killing enemies, the Pacifist gets use to Support weapon class. You also don't have to invest in offensive stats such as Damage, Melee Damage, Attack Speed, and Crit Chance in order to win the game, and can focus on their defense instead.

sections.heading: General Guide
sections.text:
When Harvesting increases by 5% at the end of each wave, this is considered a "Harvesting modification" for the purposes of the Entrepreneur's passive ability and will itself be increased by 50%. This results in an effective increase of 7.5% to Harvesting at the end of each wave even though the description for Harvesting will still display a 5% increase per wave. Combined with the 50% increased Harvesting received from level-ups and shop purchases, the Entrepreneur has a much larger than average return on Harvesting acquired early.
Since the Entrepreneur loses all material at the beginning of a wave, it is wise to spend as much material as possible before closing the shop; spending any excess material on suboptimal items or even extra re-rolls is preferable to losing that material for nothing.
In Endless Mode, the 10% Harvesting Stat lost at the end of each round is unfortunately also increased by 50%.

sections.heading: Synergies
sections.text:
Item

Effects

Notes

Crown

Harvesting increases by an additional 8% at the end of a wave

Since the end-of-wave Harvesting increases are considered "Harvesting modifications," this is in an effective increase of 12% to the multiplier for a total of 19.5% increased Harvesting at the end of each wave. The description for Harvesting will still display a 13% increase per wave.

Piggy Bank

+20% of your materials at the start of waves (stops working at wave 20)

Piggy Bank's effect stacks additively with the Entrepreneur's passive. This will increase the amount of Materials kept at the start of each wave from -100% to -80%, meaning that 20% of the Materials left over after exiting the shop will be kept instead of 0%.
However, it is generally not recommended to purchase Piggy Bank on the Entrepreneur as it will still be preferable to spend as much material as possible at each shop, resulting in a low return on investment.

sections.heading: Character
sections.text: Doctor is a playable character in the game. Individual characters differ in their Stats and traits, to encourage a variety of play styles in Brotato.

sections.heading: How to Unlock
sections.text: Heal 200 HP in one wave

sections.heading: Unlocks
sections.text:
Unlocks Medikit if you win a game with Doctor:
Medikit

sections.heading: Starting Weapons
sections.text:
Both of Doctor's starting weapons belong to the Medical class.
Scissors
 Medical Gun

sections.heading: General Guide
sections.text:
As the Doctor you have +200% attack speed with medical weapons, and -100% with all weapons in general. The attack speed is added together, so you actually have +100% with medical weapons. Due to the large negative attack speed with other weapons, it is best in most cases to stick with medical weapons. Medical weapons in general trade raw damage output for their innate life steal. In addition the medical class's bonus is HP regeneration. And the Doctor heals 2 HP instead of 1 with HP regeneration. So you'll have a lot of Healing just from your weaponry.
You will have to pick up Melee/Ranged Damage, % Damage, and crit to make up for your weapons' lack of damage output compared to other weapons. Picking up more attack speed is also fine, but it isn't a priority, since you start with +100% of it.
Armor is halved on the Doctor, but picking up the best armor options you're given can still be good. Dodge is a good option for the Doctor to mitigate damage and make the most out of your great healing potential. You might want to get your Max HP a bit higher, since you sometimes get unlucky with Dodge and take multiple hits in a row.
To avoid having to invest in both melee and ranged damage, it is often best to commit to either ranged or melee combat.
If you are using Scissors, you can pick up the third and last Medical weapon later during the game when you see it. The Circular Saw is a very powerful melee weapon that also comes with innate lifesteal, and has a large sweeping attack with a very fast attack speed.

sections.heading: Synergies
sections.text:
Item

Effect

Notes

Alien Worm

+3 Max HP+2 HP Regeneration-1 HP recovered from consumables

Alien Worm gives a lot of stats. Max HP is something you want and HP regen is doubled. Since you have so much healing, it might be worth it to discard the consumable healing.

Blood Donation

+40 HarvestingYou take 1 damage per second (does not give invulnerability time)

Blood Donation is a very powerful item if you can get it early in the game. Few characters can spare the life to use it, but this is no problem for the doctor.

Weird Ghost

+3 Max HPStart the next wave with 1 HP

The Weird ghost gives you cheap Max HP, but sets your HP to 1 during the start of the next wave. This is no problem for the Doctor, who can regain the HP without issue. But don't let your guard down.

Sad Tomato

+8 HP RegenerationStart waves with -50% HP

Sad Tomato grants a large amount of HP regeneration in trade for starting with 50% HP during the start of each wave. With additional regeneration, you'll easily get back to full health before the wave really kicks in.

Regeneration Potion

HP Regeneration is doubled when you have less than 50% health+3 HP Regeneration

The Regeneration Potion stacks with your doubled HP regeneration, and lets you heal 4 HP per tick while below 50% HP.

Ricochet

Your projectiles gain +1 bounce-25 % Damage

The Ricochet provides an additional bounce which is very powerful on ranged weapons without bounce or piercing such as the Medical Gun

Bandana

Projectiles pierce through 1 additional target-10 % Damage

The Bandana provides an additional pierce which is very powerful on ranged weapons without bounce or piercing such as the Medical Gun

sections.heading: Character
sections.text: Artificer is a playable character in the game. Individual characters differ in their Stats and traits, to encourage a variety of play styles in Brotato.

sections.heading: How to Unlock
sections.text: Kill 15 Enemies with a single explosion

sections.heading: Unlocks
sections.text:
Unlocks Explosive Shells if you win a game with Artificer:
Explosive Shells

sections.heading: Starting Weapons
sections.text:
Their arsenal consists mostly of Explosive Damage weapons and Engineer weapons. It is worth noting that turrets deal damage as normal, and if you get to tier 4 (red) wrenches, the explosive turrets will benefit from your explosive bonuses. Landmines also benefit from these. But since they are Tool weapons and not Explosive, you will be dealing 1 damage with each hit, making them significantly harder to use in the early game.

sections.heading: Character
sections.text: Streamer is a playable character in the game. Individual characters differ in their Stats and traits, to encourage a variety of play styles in Brotato.

sections.heading: How to Unlock
sections.text: Reach -20% Speed

sections.heading: Unlocks
sections.text:
Unlocks Community Support if you win a game with Streamer:
Community Support

sections.heading: Armor from Structures
sections.text: Streamer gains +2 armor for each weapons and items you have that spawn one or more Structures, including Wandering Bot. With Landmines the +2 armor is from the item itself, not for each mine on the field.

sections.heading: Generated Materials
sections.text:
While standing still, Streamer gains 3% of the materials that they're holding as income, each second. So if you have 100 materials, you'll get 3 materials each second while standing still. This has a minimum of 1 material per second, and a maximum of 25 per seconds. It takes 834 materials to reach the maximum +25 materials per second.
In the table below, you can see the mimimum materials you need to hold in order to get +1, +2, +3 etc materials per seconds.
Materials Held

0

67

100

134

167

200

234

267

300

Materials Gained per second

+1

+2

+3

+4

+5

+6

+7

+8

+9
The timer for 'standing still' resets each time you move, so you always need to stand still for a full second before you make any money.
While you can earn a lot of money this way, be careful as the negative penalties to % damage and Speed also increase. You can find yourself dealing very little damage, or moving very slowly towards the end of a wave. This can end your run easily. So keep your greed in check and stand still responsibly

sections.heading: Starting Weapons
sections.text:
Streamer is the Character that has one of the widest range of starting weapons. Since the Streamer has to stand still, some weapons can be difficult to make work. Especially melee weapons. Commonly chosen starting weapons for the streamer are: SMG or Slingshot for ranged option. Or Wrench if you want to try to go for an engineering run.

sections.heading: Character
sections.text: Glutton is a playable character in the game. Individual characters differ in their Stats and traits, to encourage a variety of play styles in Brotato.

sections.heading: How to Unlock
sections.text: Pick up 20 consumables during a run

sections.heading: Unlocks
sections.text:
Unlocks Spicy Sauce if you win a game with Glutton:
Spicy Sauce

sections.heading: Glutton
sections.text:
Glutton's gameplay revolves around eating Consumables and gaining Explosion Damage from it. You only gain the explosion damage if you're at full health. So try to stay at full HP by either avoid taking damage, or by having some Healing. Since you consume a lot of consumables, you might benefit from increasing your consumable healing with the items Lemonade or Weird Food.
You can get more consumables to drop by increasing your Luck Stat, buying the Tree or Garden item. Using the Pruner can also be quite powerful.
Pickup Range does not affect consumables while you are at full HP; you always have to walk directly onto the fruit to activate the explosion when you're unhurt.

sections.heading: Spicy Sauce
sections.text: Glutton has some weird interactions with Spicy Sauces. If you have 1 or more Spicy Sauces, the damage is simply added on to the Glutton's innate explosion ability instead of triggering on its own. This makes Spicy Sauce a quite powerful pickup on Glutton, since the sauces will trigger 100% of the time, using the bigger explosion size of Glutton's ability.

sections.heading: Crit
sections.text: Glutton's Explosion can crit for 1.5 times damage and the base crit chance is 3%. This isn't listed in the tooltips.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text:
1.1.0.0: Bonus damage against elites and bosses increased: 75% => 125%. Trees now drop +200% materials. Enemies spawned increased -75% => 70% (note that the 1.1.0.0 Patch changed the coding for how enemies spawns, affecting extreme cases like Jack. The new code made Jack face less enemies, thus gaining less income. The numbers were increased to bring Jack back to old numbers.
1.1.0.0: Enemy Health bonus decreased: +250% => 175%. Enemy Damage bonus decreased: +50% => 35%. (The 1.1.0.0 Changed how enemy Health and Damage is calculated. Now everything is multiplicative instead of additive. So this change simply compensated for that change. Jack should be about the same on Danger 5, while being easier on lower difficulties and lower accessibility settings.)
1.1.0.0: Fixed a bug that caused Jack to lose materials when more than 50 materials were on the ground.

name: Renegade

sections.heading: Character
sections.text: Renegade is a playable character in the game. Individual characters differ in their Stats and traits, to encourage a variety of play styles in Brotato.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Their damage reduction modification applies to all sources of % Damage and Explosion Damage, but does not apply to other sources like Melee and Ranged Damage.
When playing Renegade, Tier 1 items in the Shop and in Loot crates that you don't currently have a copy off will show up with Renegade's face next to it, indicating that you'll gain the +10% Damage (+2% after reduction).
Renegade's +2 projectiles is additive with other forms of multiple projectiles. So a Tier 1 Double Barrel Shotgun will shoot 6 projectiles instead of 4, which is only a +50% increase in DPS, while other weapons go from 1 to 3 projectiles, increasing their DPS by +200%.

Notes

sections.heading: Character
sections.text: Bull is a playable character in the game. Individual characters differ in their Stats and traits, to encourage a variety of play styles in Brotato.

sections.heading: How to Unlock
sections.text: Win a run in Danger 1

sections.heading: Unlocks
sections.text:
Unlocks Gnome if you win a game with Bull:
Gnome

sections.heading: Starting Weapons
sections.text: Bull has no starting Weapons, as they can't equip any.

sections.heading: Synergies
sections.text:
Item

Effects

Notes

Blood Donation

+40 HarvestingYou take 1 damage per second (does not give invulnerability time)

This is a great synergy as the Bull will explode every second while taking only 1 damage.
Warning: If you take 2 Blood Donation, it instead deals 2 damage per second, but does not cause an extra explosion.

Plastic Explosive

+25 % Explosion Size

Plastic Explosive increases Bull's explosive range by 25%, for each plastic explosive you have. 

Explosive Shells

+60 % Explosion Damage+15 % Explosion Size-15 % Damage

Bull's explosion is counted as Explosion Damage thus is affected by this item.
Explosive Shells increases Bull's explosive damage by 45% on top of the +15% explosion size. (After accounting for the downside)

Focus

+30 % Damage-3 % Attack Speed for every different weapon you have

Focus Gives you +30% damage since you have no weapons.

Regeneration Potion

HP Regeneration is doubled when you have less than 50% health+3 HP Regeneration

Regeneration Potion Doubles your HP Regeneration when you are below half health. This is amazing for Bull and can make you very hard to kill.

Rip and Tear

Enemies have a 20% chance to explode for 10 (+50%) damage when they die-5 % Crit Chance

Rip and Tear provides additional explosions when you kill enemies. You already like to pick up melee damage, and increase the damage of your explosions, so Rip and Tear will deal a lot of damage. Rip and Tear can cause chain reaction explosions to happen, clearing out the map without you having to take more damage. It also means that any tougher enemies that survived your main explosion, will take extra damage from the smaller explosions.

sections.heading: General Guide
sections.text:
The Bull cannot use weapons, but instead explode when taking damage (any damage works). So to kill enemies, the Bull must charge into them and explode.
The Bull cannot survive being hit every second, so the trick is to know when and where to explode. Try to kill as many enemies as possible with each explosion, so you take less damage overall. Walking around enemies tends to make them group up.
Some enemies deal more damage than others. Look for those Enemies that deal low damage. Such as: or
To kill trees, simply lure enemies next to the tree and take damage so you explode near the tree.
Bull can have difficulties killing elites or bosses, as they deal higher damage compared to other enemies, so Bull cannot charge them often; try to run into other enemies near them to damage them instead, or just abandon attempting to kill them altogether, and just try to survive the wave.

sections.heading: Stats
sections.text:
Armor and HP Regeneration are very important for sustaining all the damage Bull takes throughout a wave.
Speed is also very important as it allows you to pick your fight and choose when to take damage.
Max HP can also be useful as the player needs to be able to take several hits without dying. But it doesn't let you stay in the fight in the long run. 3 Max HP is 3 Max HP, but 3 HP regeneration will regenerate about 16 HP over the 60 seconds of a wave.
Dodge is also useful if the player has Blood Donation or to slow down the rate of damage they take, but be mindful that it will reduce the amount of explosions- Bull needs to take damage to explode!
Your Explosions scale with Melee Damage, Ranged Damage, Elemental Damage. You often get double the melee damage for the same price, since melee weapons scale higher than ranged weapons, but for Bull, both give the same damage, so you will often find the best deals in melee damage; in the same vein, items that give multiple e.g. Alloy are very valuable as Bull can double (or even triple) dip off the damage increases.
Dynamite and Explosive Shells are great ways to scale Percentage Damage since all your damage is done with explosions.
Crit Chance allows explosions to deal 1.5x critical damage. This is half the bonus damage of the normal 2.0x modifier that most weapons have. Your explosions often already kill weaker enemies in one hit, so in those cases crit won't be useful.
Life Steal - Your explosions don't trigger life steal.
Attack Speed - You have no way of attacking.
Range - Your explosions aren't affected by range.
Harvesting and Luck are about the same importance as for other characters. Bull doesn't have to spend materials on weapons in the early game, and is generally strong during the early waves. Use this window to invest in your economy.

sections.heading: Damage
sections.text:
The Masochist begins with -100% Damage, meaning that most weapons will deal only 1 damage until the player takes enough hits to stack the +5% damage buff.
Taking 20 hits (regardless of how much damage each hit deals) will completely offset this initial downside with +100% Damage. Additional hits taken beyond this point will continue to stack the Damage bonus into the positives.
It is better to be hit by Enemies that deal low damage. Such as:

sections.heading: Stats
sections.text:
The following stats will help to mitigate damage taken from enemies while intentionally taking hits:
Max HP increases the amount of damage that can be taken.
HP Regeneration and Life Steal increase HP recovery.
Luck will increase the rate of health pickup drops from Enemies - another effective but less reliable source of HP recovery.
Armor is very important to reduce the damage taken from each hit.
Dodge can be helpful to reduce the frequency of hits taken, but will also reduce the rate at which Damage will be stacked.
Ideally, Dodge should be kept as low as possible unless Blood Donation has been acquired while other defensive stats should be prioritized.
Turrets and mines are not affected by the Damage stat. This means that their damage will not be reduced by the initial -100% Damage debuff, but they will also not benefit from the Damage buff received when taking damage.

sections.heading: Synergies
sections.text:
Item

Effects

Notes

Blood Donation

+40 HarvestingYou take 1 damage per second (does not give invulnerability time)

Blood Donation deals 1 damage to the player every second while giving +40 to Harvesting.
The damage dealt by Blood Donation will trigger the Masochist's passive effect, providing +5% Damage every second when it triggers. This provides a way to quickly stack Damage each wave while taking significantly less damage to do so. For comparison, the rate at which the Masochist's Damage increases with Blood Donation will be five times the rate provided by Wisdom, completely counteracting the initial -100% Damage debuff within the first 20 seconds of each wave and adding an additional +100% Damage for each additional 20 seconds that pass.
The first 10 points of HP Regeneration will provide enough HP recovery to offset Blood Donation's damage, and the Masochist begins with 20 - making it safe to purchase at any time. Once acquired, investing in Dodge may also become more valuable to reduce how many hits are taken from Enemies since the damage from Blood Donation cannot be dodged and will provide a sizable boost on its own.
Obtaining a second Blood Donation will not increase the rate at which Damage increases but will increase the damage taken each second to 2 while providing another +40 Harvesting.

Bloody Hand

+10 % Life Steal+2 % Damage for every 1 % Life Steal you have You take 1 damage per second (does not give invulnerability time)

Bloody Hand provides the same benefits that Blood Donation does, triggering Masochist's passive effect once per second.
Depending on how much lifesteal you already have, Bloody Hand can provide extra healing and Damage instead of providing harvesting.

sections.heading: Shopping
sections.text:
For the Demon, items cost Max HP instead of Materials. At the end of each wave, half your materials are converted to Max HP at the rate of 13 Materials = 1 Max HP. Items in the shop have their price changed into Max HP at the rate of 20 Materials = 1 Max HP Rounded up. This means that if an item costs 1-20 Materials, it will cost 1 HP. 21-40 Materials will cost 2 HP and so on.
Coupon works like normal on Demon. It reduces the cost of the items, and if this pushes the item below a 20 material threshold, the Max HP cost goes down by 1.
So this is why a Torch costs 1 HP in the first shop, but Ghost Axe cost 2 HP.
During the early shops, items that grant an increase to your Max HP can often be bought for less HP than they grant. Take for example Broken Mouth, Cake, or Acid. This can give Demon free Max HP which also means free money during the early game. The items have downsides, but the free Max HP is more than often worth it in the early game.
The Demon is also able to use their starting 10 HP to shop with, and this allows the demon to make out like a bandit from the first few shops, while items are cheap. Experienced players sometimes play Demon with 0-1 HP for the first 6-9 waves.
Remember that buying out the shop gives you a free reroll. It can sometimes be worth it to buy a weapon or item you don't want, and recycle the weapon for a free reroll.

sections.heading: Shopping Interactions
sections.text:
If Demon buys Handcuffs, the cost of buying Handcuffs will be deducted from their HP first, then their HP will be capped at the lower value.
If Demon has Padding, Stone Skin, you can sometimes encounter not being able to buy items in the shop despite having the HP needed. This is because you can't spend the temporary HP gained from those items.

sections.heading: Hidden Ability
sections.text:
Vagabond has an unlisted bonus. Vagabond cannot find weapons of a type they already have of a lower tier. And if they have a Tier 4 Weapon, they cannot find that weapon at all in the shop.
So if you have a Tier 2 Knife, you cannot find Tier 1 Knives in the Shop. And if you have a Tier 4 Knife, you cannot find more Knives in the Shop at all.
In general this helps Vagabond find weapons and upgrade their weapons just a bit faster.

sections.heading: Builder's Turret
sections.text:
Builder's Turret has 4 tiers. Fulfilling the requirements levels up the turret to the next tier.
As 5 uncollected materials are needed to increase Structure Range by +1%, each tier costs 150 uncollected materials to level up, for a total of 450 materials across all tiers.

name: Gangster

sections.heading: Character
sections.text: Gangster is a playable character in the game. Individual characters differ in their Stats and traits, to encourage a variety of play styles in Brotato.

sections.heading: How to Unlock
sections.text: Reroll 10 times in a single shop

sections.heading: Unlocks
sections.text:
Unlocks Grenade Launcher if you win a game with Gangster:
Grenade Launcher

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Gangster always faces Elite Waves and won't see any Horde Waves when playing on higher Danger Levels
Gangster cannot find items with the Lock tag. This is currently just Fish Hook.

sections.heading: Stealing Items
sections.text:
Gangster can steal one item during each shop. But in return you cannot lock items. (They use the same button). Efficient use of the Steal ability will put you ahead on items, even with the 20% increased shop prices.
However, stealing an item comes with the risk of spawning an elite during the next wave.
Chance of Elite spawning: Wave ⋅ Item Tier ⋅ Item Price / 120
Stealing chance is rounded to nearest 0.1% and cannot go above 95% chance to spawn an elite.
Stealing chance is affected by Shop Price, so Coupon decreases the chance. But remember that Gangster starts with +20% Shop prices.
So if you

sections.heading: Spawning an Elite
sections.text:
When you steal an item and get caught, the Elite symbol shows up, and a growl can be heard. During the next wave, an additional elite will show up. If you get caught before an Elite Wave or before the Bosses, you're in for a hard fight. The elite will show up in addition to the default elite or boss/es.
Killing any Elite, spawned from the Shop, or one of the baseline elites from the Elite Wave, will increase the HP of any future elite/boss by 15%, and their damage by 7.5%
So if you kill the 3 Elites and maybe 2 extra that you've spawned with the shop, that's +75% HP and +37.5% Damage for the Bosses.

sections.heading: Elite and Bosses HP Modifiers
sections.text:
In addition to Gangster's ability to increase Elite/Boss health, there's also some modifiers that are present in all games for all characters.
Elites have reduced health on earlier waves: 50% HP from wave 1-8, 75% HP wave 9-12, and 100% HP wave 13+
These are multiplicative with your +15%/+7.5% on kill ability.
This makes it quite possible to actually kill elites in the early game.
Also remember that Elite's HP scale linearly with waves. See Enemies.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Hiker's unique mechanic is it's ability to gain materials and Max HP by taking steps during a wave.
Some notes about the mechanic
By default character takes 3.33... steps each second while moving through arena.
Steps scale with the Speed stat. Having 10 Speed means character walks (3.33... + 10%) = 3.66... steps each second.
Due to Hiker's innate -5% Speed, he starts the game by walking 3.166... steps / s.
Walking into a wall doesn't give any steps. Only walking that actually covers distance counts for a step.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Romantic's Charm will not be applied via Burning Damage (including Incendiary Turret's Burn) nor via random-target effects like Cyberball.
Structures (such as Turret or Landmines), however, can apply charm when they hit a target (which does include the non-burn part of an Incendiary Turret's hit). Spawned Projectiles (e.g. Alien Eyes) will also apply the effect.

sections.heading: Cacti Club
sections.text: Cacti Club is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Cacti Club is a Melee Weapon. Melee weapons have two different attack types, thrust and swing, both are capable of hitting multiple enemies. Melee weapons' Range only increases or decreases by half the amount of your Range stat. The attack speed of melee weapons depends on their range. Increasing the range slightly decreases the Attack Speed, but increases the area hit by the weapon. Negative range has the inverse effect.

sections.heading: Class
sections.text:
Cacti Club has the Weapon Class(es) of: Primitive, Heavy.
Weapons in the same class gain bonuses, and are more likely to be found in the Shop.

sections.heading: Chopper
sections.text: Chopper is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Chopper is a Melee Weapon. Melee weapons have two different attack types, thrust and swing, both are capable of hitting multiple enemies. Melee weapons' Range only increases or decreases by half the amount of your Range stat. The attack speed of melee weapons depends on their range. Increasing the range slightly decreases the Attack Speed, but increases the area hit by the weapon. Negative range has the inverse effect.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
The Chopper provides additional healing with consumables. Having 6 choppers gives +6 to consumable healing, or even +12 if all of them are tier 4. Increasing your Luck stat increases the amount of consumables dropped.
The Chopper scales its damage by 20% of your Max HP, so it might be worth it to pick up more Max HP than you otherwise would.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
The Circular Saw is a powerful weapon, which lowest level is tier 2. The only one that can start with the Circular Saw is King, beside that it can be found in shop. The Circular Saw come with innate Life Steal. In addition, both of its weapon classes, Blade and Medical, also come with Life steal and HP Regeneration.
The low base damage, but high melee scaling means that having some Melee Damage is important for dealing damage with the Circular Saw.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
The Drill is a Legendary Weapon that comes with a powerful 2.5x Crit and a 100% chance to gain materials on a Critical Hit kill. This makes it basically a much stronger version of the Thief Dagger.
In addition, it gains you 10% Attack Speed for every 5 Seconds in a wave.

name: Excalibur

sections.heading: Excalibur
sections.text: Excalibur is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Excalibur is a Melee Weapon. Melee weapons have two different attack types, thrust and swing, both are capable of hitting multiple enemies. Melee weapons' Range only increases or decreases by half the amount of your Range stat. The attack speed of melee weapons depends on their range. Increasing the range slightly decreases the Attack Speed, but increases the area hit by the weapon. Negative range has the inverse effect.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Excalibur is quite an extraordinary weapon. It boasts an impressive 200 Base damage, 200% Melee Damage and Max HP Scaling. It has a low cooldown and a 2.5 Critical Hit multiplier.
Together, these stats make Excalibur the highest Damage per Second Melee weapon by far. However, it comes at a cost. Excalibur gives you -3 Armor for each weapon you have. So for most builds, this means -18 armor for 6 weapons. However, some characters are able to evade this downside.
One Armed only has one weapon, meaning you're just taking a single -3 to your armor.
 Ghost starts with -100 Armor, and due to how negative armor works, there is barely any difference between -100 and -118 Armor.
In addition, the Sword is very expensive with a base price of 260, it also has the Legendary Weapon Class, meaning you'll lose Max HP from having more than one Legendary Weapon.
Despite the weapon class of Legendary and unique Name of the Sword you can actually have multiple Excaliburs.

sections.heading: Ethereal Weapon
sections.text:
Ethereal Weapons grant stats whenever you kill the listed amount of enemies with the weapon.
The kill count is separate for each weapon, and the count resets between waves.
There is no limit to how many stats you can gain in a wave with a ghost weapon. In Endless Mode this can get into the thousands.
The gained stats are permanent and will persist even if you sell the weapon that gained the stats.

sections.heading: Knife
sections.text: Knife is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Knife is a Melee Weapon. Melee weapons have two different attack types, thrust and swing, both are capable of hitting multiple enemies. Melee weapons' Range only increases or decreases by half the amount of your Range stat. The attack speed of melee weapons depends on their range. Increasing the range slightly decreases the Attack Speed, but increases the area hit by the weapon. Negative range has the inverse effect.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
The Knife is a weapon based around critical hits, and can reach some of the highest single target damage per second of any melee weapon in the game. But it takes time for the knife to reach that point.
The early game can be rough, as critical hits can be quite unreliable when you have 20% to 30% chance to crit. Either the hit doesn't crit, and doesn't deal enough damage, or it crits and deals too much, wasting some of the damage.
The knife reaches a Critical Damage modifier of 4x at tier 4, compared to the normal modifier of 2x which most weapons have. Which means that Crit Chance provides 3 times the increase to your damage output compared to a regular weapon with a 2x modifier.
The tier 4 knife has a base Crit Chance of 50%, and having 6 Precise weapons grants another 15% Crit Chance, so you only need an additional 35% to reach 100% chance to crit on a hit.
The Knife has a low base damage even at higher tiers compared to most other weapons, but it scales well with Melee Damage.

sections.heading: Lightning Shiv
sections.text:
Whenever the Lightning shiv hits an enemy with the shiv, a lightning projectile is spawned, that targets another random enemy anywhere on the map. You can spawn multiple projectiles with a single attack if you hit multiple targets.
The Tooltip of the Lightning in the game claims that the shiv bounces 1 time more than it actually does.
The Spawned Projectiles of the Lightning Shiv can Crit and Life Steal and can inflict Burn if you have one or more Scared Sausage.

name: Scythe

sections.heading: Scythe
sections.text: Scythe is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Scythe is a Melee Weapon. Melee weapons have two different attack types, thrust and swing, both are capable of hitting multiple enemies. Melee weapons' Range only increases or decreases by half the amount of your Range stat. The attack speed of melee weapons depends on their range. Increasing the range slightly decreases the Attack Speed, but increases the area hit by the weapon. Negative range has the inverse effect.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Scythe is the only weapon that actively hurts you, much like Blood Donation and Bloody Hand.
Unlike other Ethereal Weapons, the Scythe doesn't give you permanent stats from killing enemies, but instead gives temporary Damage whenever you take damage. The stats from the Scythe only lasts until the end of the wave.

sections.heading: Wrench
sections.text: Wrench is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Wrench is a Melee Weapon. Melee weapons have two different attack types, thrust and swing, both are capable of hitting multiple enemies. Melee weapons' Range only increases or decreases by half the amount of your Range stat. The attack speed of melee weapons depends on their range. Increasing the range slightly decreases the Attack Speed, but increases the area hit by the weapon. Negative range has the inverse effect.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
The Wrench is mostly used in Engineering builds for its special ability to spawn a turret. It comes with a powerful knockback that can keep you safe while the turrets deal damage. The wrench in itself does not have good potential for dealing damage.
When you are buying more Wrenches in the Shop it is a good idea to consider which of your 6 Wrenches you want to combine into higher tiers. Since each tier of Wrench comes with a different turret, you should consider which turrets are best for the coming waves and with the items you have.

name: Gatling Laser

sections.heading: Gatling Laser
sections.text: Gatling Laser is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Gatling Laser is a Ranged Weapon. Ranged weapons shoot projectiles that travel up to the total Range. Unless the weapon has piercing, the projectiles stop at the first enemy that is hit.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text: Gatling Laser has the fastest possible base attack speed of any weapon, with a base cooldown stat of 2, and a recoil duration of 0.02, giving it a base cooldown of 0.073. This matches Chain Gun and Tier IV Minigun. These three guns have the same attack speed. Combined with its good ranged damage scaling and 3x crit multiplier, Gatling Laser is capable of dealing massive damage.

sections.heading: Ethereal Weapon
sections.text:
Ethereal Weapons grant stats whenever you kill the listed amount of enemies with the weapon.
The kill count is separate for each weapon, and the count resets between waves.
There is no limit to how many stats you can gain in a wave with a ghost weapon. In Endless Mode this can get into the thousands.
The gained stats are permanent and will persist even if you sell the weapon that gained the stats.

sections.heading: Revolver
sections.text: Revolver is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Revolver is a Ranged Weapon. Ranged weapons shoot projectiles that travel up to the total Range. Unless the weapon has piercing, the projectiles stop at the first enemy that is hit.

sections.heading: Reload
sections.text:
The Revolver has to reload every time it has fired 6 shots. This reload time is about 4-5 times the normal cooldown, and scales with Weapon Tier and Attack Speed.
The average cooldown is: 0.70s/0.67s/0.63s/0.60s
The listed DPS of this weapon includes the reload cooldown.

sections.heading: Shredder
sections.text: Shredder is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Shredder is a Ranged Weapon. Ranged weapons shoot projectiles that travel up to the total Range. Unless the weapon has piercing, the projectiles stop at the first enemy that is hit.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
The Shredder is a powerful explosive gun that specialises in dealing damage to large groups of enemies. But it can struggle with dealing single target damage.
The Shredder can explode on each piercing hit. Since each shot can pierce 3 times, which is 4 hits total, it can explode up to 4 times. This also means that enemies in crowds can take up to 4 times the damage, if they are hit by all 4 explosions.
As an Explosive weapon, Shredder benefits from Dynamite and Plastic Explosive.

sections.heading: SMG
sections.text: SMG is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.SMG is a Ranged Weapon. Ranged weapons shoot projectiles that travel up to the total Range. Unless the weapon has piercing, the projectiles stop at the first enemy that is hit.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
The SMG is a fast shooting gun with good single target damage output. This makes it great at quickly reacting to enemies that charge or spawn next to you.
The SMG has a low base damage, but a high scaling with Ranged Damage. Neither the Base damage nor Attack Speed of the SMG increase significantly as you upgrade the gun to higher tiers, but its scaling with Range damage Increases with each tier. So a good amount of Ranged Damage is important.
The SMG's shots are quite inaccurate and might miss their target at longer range.
Due to the fast firing speed, SMG's can trigger Life Steal very often, making it a powerful option for your healing.
The SMG can struggle with clearing large groups of enemies. You can solve this issue by getting items such as Bandana, Baby with a Beard, or Ricochet. Or you can pick up another gun with better damage against big groups of enemies like Double Barrel Shotgun or Shredder.
The SMG has a critical hit multiplier of 1.5x instead of the regular 2x. This means Crit Chance only has half of the increase on your damage output compared to most other weapons. Take this into account when investing into the stat.

sections.heading: Sniper Gun
sections.text: Sniper Gun is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Sniper Gun is a Ranged Weapon. Ranged weapons shoot projectiles that travel up to the total Range. Unless the weapon has piercing, the projectiles stop at the first enemy that is hit.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
The Sniper Gun is a tier-3 minimum; No characters can start with it, and it can only be found in the shop.
The Sniper Gun has higher critical damage than regular weapons and benefit greatly from getting Crit Chance, and the projectiles spawned from the main shot have the same 3x/4x Crit multiplier. The slow attack with high damage and extra critical damage means that the Sniper Gun can reach numbers over 2000 damage per shot in the late game.
Its damage scales much better with Range rather than the normal Ranged Damage. These are the same stats as the Crossbow scale with, making the Sniper Gun a possible late game addition to any Crossbow-focused build.
The Sniper Gun benefits immensely from the items Bandana and Ricochet, as it not only makes both the main projectile and the spawned projectiles Bounce/Pierce, but the main projectile spawns more projectiles every time it hits another target after bouncing or piercing.

name: Brick

sections.heading: Brick
sections.text: Brick is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Brick is a Melee Weapon. Melee weapons have two different attack types, thrust and swing, both are capable of hitting multiple enemies. Melee weapons' Range only increases or decreases by half the amount of your Range stat. The attack speed of melee weapons depends on their range. Increasing the range slightly decreases the Attack Speed, but increases the area hit by the weapon. Negative range has the inverse effect.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Brick is the weapon with the lowest base cost in the game. It has quite impressive damage, especially for its cost. But these impressive stacks comes at the cost of the Brick having a 1% chance to break when hitting an enemy.
When the brick breaks, it drops 10/30/60/120 Materials.
The Tier 2 Brick drops more Materials than two Tier 1 Bricks. So you can combine Bricks to Tier 2 to make more profit.
The breaking is per 1% target hit. If you swing towards a large group of enemies, it might break upon hitting the first target, letting the rest live.
When the Brick breaks, it drops Materials, Which give both Money and Experience
Brick is affected by the Materials drop stat. So characters like Streamer or Farmer only get 50% materials from Bricks. Characters with -50% materials from enemies, like Explorer still get the full amount.
On the other hand Jack gets 3x the materials from bricks and Buccaneer gets 2x the materials, making Brick an interesting option for them.

sections.heading: Class
sections.text:
Brick has the Weapon Class(es) of: Blunt.
Weapons in the same class gain bonuses, and are more likely to be found in the Shop.

name: Chainsaw

sections.heading: Chainsaw
sections.text: Chainsaw is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Chainsaw is a Melee Weapon. Melee weapons have two different attack types, thrust and swing, both are capable of hitting multiple enemies. Melee weapons' Range only increases or decreases by half the amount of your Range stat. The attack speed of melee weapons depends on their range. Increasing the range slightly decreases the Attack Speed, but increases the area hit by the weapon. Negative range has the inverse effect.

sections.heading: Class

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Chainsaw is quite a unique weapon. It has triple scaling, it has to reload, and it deals Health Percent Damage.
It is also the only Tool weapon that doesn't spawn structures. It can be a way from engineering builds to deal damage. A character like Dwarf that ends up with a ton of Engineering and Melee Damage can deal great damage with Chainsaw. The life steal scaling also makes it an interesting choice for lifesteal based characters like Sick or Vampire.

sections.heading: Reload
sections.text: The Chainsaw has to reload every time it has attacked 30/40 times. The reload time is 1.83s/1.52s and scales with Attack Speed.

sections.heading: Health Percent Damage
sections.text: And finally it has a special ability to deal Health Percent Damage on each hit, making it a great weapon for slicing up Elites and Bosses.

sections.heading: Cannot be modified
sections.text:
You cannot increase the damage done by HP%DMG. It isn't modified by anything.
The Damage dealt by HP%DMG isn't increased by % Damage, or multiplied by Critical Hits. It isn't increased by Silver Bullet, or Small Fish.

sections.heading: Cursed
sections.text: When Cursed, the HP%DMG damage effect is multiplied by the Curse boost. So 10%/1% (Regular/Elite) damage per hit can be increased all the way to 21%/2.1%. Note that the 10 to 1 ratio between damage to regular enemies and damage to elite/bosses is kept.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the Game.

name: Harpoon Gun

sections.heading: Harpoon Gun
sections.text: Harpoon Gun is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Harpoon Gun is a Ranged Weapon. Ranged weapons shoot projectiles that travel up to the total Range. Unless the weapon has piercing, the projectiles stop at the first enemy that is hit.

sections.heading: Class
sections.text:
Harpoon Gun has the Weapon Class(es) of: Naval, Gun.
Weapons in the same class gain bonuses, and are more likely to be found in the Shop.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
The Harpoon Gun is a quite unique gun. It scales with both Melee Damage and Ranged Damage, and pulls enemies that are hit by it. It also slows enemies hit by it.
Increasing your Knockback Stat has the reverse effect on harpoon gun, making it have a higher negative value. So if you have +10 Knockback, Harpoon Gun will have -30-10 = -40 knockback.
Harpoon Gun ignores 90% of an enemy's Knockback resistance, meaning it can pull enemies that are otherwise really hard to knock back. However, it cannot pull enemies that are knockback immune, such as elites and bosses.
Another hidden ability of the harpoon gun: Enemies pulled at least 200 distance by the negative knockback have their damage reduced by 90% for 1 second.
The Character Diver starts with a Harpoon Gun and it costs 1 gold in the Shop and when recycling for Diver.
 Generalist might find the Harpoon extra useful, since they benefit greatly from the dual scaling.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the Game.

notes:
The Harpoon Gun is a quite unique gun. It scales with both Melee Damage and Ranged Damage, and pulls enemies that are hit by it. It also slows enemies hit by it.
Increasing your Knockback Stat has the reverse effect on harpoon gun, making it have a higher negative value. So if you have +10 Knockback, Harpoon Gun will have -30-10 = -40 knockback.
Harpoon Gun ignores 90% of an enemy's Knockback resistance, meaning it can pull enemies that are otherwise really hard to knock back. However, it cannot pull enemies that are knockback immune, such as elites and bosses.
Another hidden ability of the harpoon gun: Enemies pulled at least 200 distance by the negative knockback have their damage reduced by 90% for 1 second.
The Character Diver starts with a Harpoon Gun and it costs 1 gold in the Shop and when recycling for Diver.
 Generalist might find the Harpoon extra useful, since they benefit greatly from the dual scaling.

sections.heading: Hiking Pole
sections.text: Hiking Pole is a weapon that can be bought from the Shop. Weapons are primary damage dealers and most Characters can hold up to 6 weapons.Hiking Pole is a Melee Weapon. Melee weapons have two different attack types, thrust and swing, both are capable of hitting multiple enemies. Melee weapons' Range only increases or decreases by half the amount of your Range stat. The attack speed of melee weapons depends on their range. Increasing the range slightly decreases the Attack Speed, but increases the area hit by the weapon. Negative range has the inverse effect.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.text:
Hiking Pole's unique mechanic is it's ability to increase character's range (and consequentially Pole's damage) by taking steps during a wave.
Some notes about the mechanic
By default character takes 3.33... steps each second while moving through arena.
Steps scale with the Speed stat. Having 10 Speed means character walks (3.33... + 10%) = 3.66... steps each second.
Walking into a wall doesn't give any steps. Only walking that actually covers distance counts for a step.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Adrenaline is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Adrenaline is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Adrenaline
sections.text: Adrenaline has 50% chance to provide you with a 5 heal when you dodge an attack. This can be a powerful way of healing if you have the maximum amount of dodge, 60% or close to that amount. This can be especially useful on Melee builds that are more likely to get hit often such as Brawler's fist build. The Character Ghost can reach up to 90% dodge chance, making Adrenaline a powerful option for healing.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Alien Baby is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Enemy Health
sections.text:
The +10% Enemy health from Alien Baby is multiplicative with other factors such as Endless Mode, Danger Levels, and Accessibility Settings.
However, it is additive with other "+ Enemy Health" effects from characters such as Jack or Diver. This makes Baby Alien a more attractive choice for those characters.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Downside changed from: +8% Enemy Speed to +10% Enemy Health

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Alien Eyes is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Alien Eyes
sections.text:
Alien Eyes' projectiles can Crit, Lifesteal, pierce, bounce do "normal" attack things. And it can also set enemies on fire with Scared Sausage.
Additional projectiles from playing Renegade or having the Seashell item does not affect Alien eyes.
The damage scale with your Max HP and your % Damage
Each additional Alien Eyes increases the amount of projectiles fired by +6.

name: Anvil

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Anvil is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Anvil is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Anvil will upgrade a random weapon of yours each time you enter a shop. Once there are no weapons to upgrade, it will give you 2 Armor instead.
When acquired on Wildling, your weapons cannot be upgraded above Tier 2 by the Anvil, so Anvil will give you armor instead.
Arms Dealer will lose their weapons first when entering the shop, so will always get the 2 Armor.

name: Axolotl

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Axolotl is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Axolotl is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Axolotl is an extremely polarizing item. It can easily break the game and make the run an easy win, or a terrible loss.
It switches your highest stat, and your lowest positive stat. This can lead to you being close to invincible if you switch a large amount of for example Harvesting into Armor, or lead to you instantly killing everything if switched into for example Ranged Damage
Axolotl is especially coded to only show up 50% less compared to other legendary items.
Axolotl is also the only item in the game that cannot be locked in the shop. This was done to prevent the player from being able to buy items and upgrades to the point where they could choose the stats the Axolotl would change.
When Cursed, the Axolotl gives +5 to both stats that it is switched. The +5 is then boosted by the item's curse.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

notes:
Axolotl is an extremely polarizing item. It can easily break the game and make the run an easy win, or a terrible loss.
It switches your highest stat, and your lowest positive stat. This can lead to you being close to invincible if you switch a large amount of for example Harvesting into Armor, or lead to you instantly killing everything if switched into for example Ranged Damage
Axolotl is especially coded to only show up 50% less compared to other legendary items.
Axolotl is also the only item in the game that cannot be locked in the shop. This was done to prevent the player from being able to buy items and upgrades to the point where they could choose the stats the Axolotl would change.
When Cursed, the Axolotl gives +5 to both stats that it is switched. The +5 is then boosted by the item's curse.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Baby Elephant is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Baby Elephant
sections.text:
Baby Elephant's damage is a direct effect, does not crit, lifesteal, nor do "normal" attack things.
Notably, the damage dealt by the Baby Elephant is affected by the player's damage modifier. So, a player with 100 Luck and +50% damage will deal 37.5 damage on a successful trigger.

sections.heading: Scaling
sections.text:
If you have more than one, each Baby Elephant will trigger independantly each time you pick up a material.
So for example if you have 3 Baby Elephants and pick up a material, an enemy is chosen, and then the game rolls a 100 sided die three times. For each succesful roll, the target takes damage. So with 3 elephants that deal 10 damage on hit, the target could take 0, 10, 20, or 30 damage.

name: Baby Squid

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Baby Squid is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Baby Squid is a quite powerful HP Regeneration option. It gives +1 HP Regeneration whenever you level up. As such the item gives more stats the ealier in the run you get it, and it works quite well with XP Gain or Mutant because you'll gain more levels.
Baby Squid is quite similar to Decomposing Flesh.

name: Baby With A Beard
page_title: Baby with a Beard - Brotato Wiki

sections.heading: Baby with a Beard
sections.text:
Baby with a Beard's projectiles can Crit, Lifesteal, pierce, bounce do "normal" attack things. It can also spread flames if you have Scared Sausage.
The item scales with Ranged damage and % Damage. Since the item has no base damage, Range damage is needed to make Baby with a Beard deal good damage.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Baby with a Beard is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Bag is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Bag
sections.text:
Bag provides 15 Materials for each Loot Crate you pick up. You can have up to 3 bags, providing you a total of +45 materials per loot crate. Since multiple crates can be found per wave, bag can end up becoming a significant boost to your economy.
Unlike the Materials gained by Harvesting, Bag doesn't give Experience. It only gives you the Currency Part of Materials.
You can increase the amount of Loot Crates you find by buying the Tree items, as trees have a decent chance to drop crates, and by increasing your Luck stat.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Bait is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Bait
sections.text:
Bait provides a significant amount of Damage at a cheap cost, but it comes with a risk.
At the start of the next wave after aquiring a Bait, between 7 and 8 Lamprey enemies will spawn at the start of the wave to chase you down.
The Lampreys are quite dangerous enemies that will charge you while firing projectiles. However, if you manage to kill them, you'll earn yourself an additional 7-8 Materials, making Bait even more Cost efficient.
Will you take the Bait?

name: Ball And Chain

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Ball and Chain is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Ball and Chain is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
The Ball and Chain comes with quite a unique downside. It gives your weapons a minimum cooldown of 0.75 seconds. This doesn't seem to account for the animation time of melee weapons. So for melee weapons, their minimum cooldown with Ball and Call will be more than 0.75 seconds depending on how long the range of the weapon is.
The Stats are great for its price, so it is worthwhile to consider on characters with Attack speed penalties such as Buccaneer, Dwarf, or Ogre. And in general if you're using slower weapons like Laser Gun or Hammer
Despite the Tooltip saying weapons, the Ball and Chain does limit the attack speed of Structures. This includes both regular Turret and the Builder's Turret.
A Cursed Ball and Chain will have a minimum cooldown that is lower than 0.75 seconds. This opens the Cursed Ball and Chain up to being useful on more builds.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Bandana is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Bandana
sections.text:
The Bandana increases the amount of pierce for projectiles by 1. This includes ranged weapons and projectiles spawned by weapons such as Cacti Club.
It is a very useful item to help single target ranged weapons keep up with large hordes of enemies.
Projectiles have a default damage falloff of 50%, unless the weapon has piercing by default such as the Laser Gun. The piercing from the bandana uses the same damage falloff as the innate piercing, and is also affected by Pumpkin
Projectiles with both bounce and pierce will only pierce after they're done bouncing.

name: Barnacle

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Barnacle is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Barnacle is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Barnacle increases the stats given by Upgrades, similarly to Captain. The increased amount will show up directly on the upgrade. So a Tier 3 Armor upgrade will show up as +4 instead of +3. Still the Purple color.
It also gives +1 Curse When leveling up. This can either be considered a positive bonus, or a big downside depending on your build and opinion on the Curse stat.
Since Barnacle gives value upon leveling up, it is better the earlier you get it. You can get even more levels by buying XP Gain items.
Upgrade Values are rounded down. So picking a 2 Armor upgrade doesn't give you 2.7 Armor. It just gives 2 armor.
Barnacle stacks Additively with the +100% from Captain for at total of +135% upgrade stats.

name: Black Flag

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Black Flag is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Black Flag is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Black flag is quite the item. It increases the amount of enemies, makes them deal more damage and have more health, and then the +5 Curse means more cursed enemies making it even harder.
However +10% more enemies also means more Materials Dropped. And the +1 money per kill for cursed enemies means a lot of gold on runs with a lot of Curse.
The Cursed version of Black flag gives +2 or even sometimes +3 gold per kill and can change the entire run. It is a very powerful item for Endless Mode, where 100% of the enemies will end up being cursed once you get to the later waves.
Black Flag is better the earlier you get it, as you have more waves to earn a profit. Characters that are built around Curse, such as Sailor or Creature should highly consider picking it up.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Blood Donation is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Effect
sections.text:
Blood Donation provides a large amount of Harvesting for its price, but comes at the cost of dealing 1 damage to you per second. The effect can easily kill you over the duration of a wave if you don't have sufficient healing to counteract the damage.
The Self-Damage of Blood Donation triggers on hit effects of characters such as Bull and Masochist, providing a powerful synergy. But it also leads to terrible results if combined with Weird Ghost, Tardigrade, or Triangle of Power

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Bloody Hand is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Bloody Hand is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Bloody Hand
sections.text: Bloody Hand Provides a large amount of Life Steal and Damage at the cost of dealing 1 damage to you each second.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
The self-damage of Bloody Hand triggers on-hit effects of characters such as Bull and Masochist; this can lead to strong synergy.
The self-damage will usually kill you if you hold a Weird Ghost, which puts your current HP to 1 at the beginning of the wave.
The effect from Tardigrade is wasted by the self-harm.
Triangle of Power should not be used with this item, as you would lose 2 %Damage per second.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: "Bloody Hand" is one of several easter-eggs that represent Creator, who shared Brotato-Gameplay on YouTube or Twitch respectively and thus helped popularize it. The item-icon is a representation of the channel-icon of german YouTube creator "HandOfBlood", whose channel-name itself is a reference to the Song "Hand of Blood" from "Bullet for my Valentine".

sections.heading: Boxing Glove
sections.text:
Boxing Glove increases your Knockback by +3. This can be a powerful, but niche pickup in the right situation where you are in great need for knockback.
For example, on Wave 11 The Enemy spawns. These will speed up until they hit you. But they are extra susceptible to being knocked back. So if you don't have enough damage output to kill them fast, and your weapons don't naturally have good knockback, Boxing Glove can be a powerful pickup to help you in that specific situation.

sections.text: Community Support is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Community Support is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Community Support
sections.text:
Community Support is a powerful item that allows you to trade some of your defense into offense, similar to Glass Cannon. Community Support requires a significant amount of enemies to be on the map for it to be efficient for its high cost. But the strength of the item is that if you fall behind, it becomes stronger and helps you deal with the enemies. This is particularly useful when dealing with Horde waves or waves with large amounts of enemies, such as wave 9.
Charmed enemies (via Romantic or Flute) do still count for Community Support.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Coupon is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Coupon
sections.text:
Coupon reduces the Items Price secondary stat by 5%.
It is the only item in the game that modifies shop prices.
Shop price reductions stack additively, and the current total can be seen in the secondary stats tab, as % Items Price. You can stack up to 5 Coupons for a maximum 25% price reduction. (Items will cost 75% of the default price.
The highest cost reduction possible is 50%, on the character Entrepreneur and having the maximum amount of 5 coupons.
Coupon does not modify the cost of rerolls in the Shop or rerolling Upgrades.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text: The Coupon is an efficient economic item that provides a sizeable net gain in most runs, unless bought very late (outside of endless mode). It is generally considered one of the better items in the game, and is nearly always bought in shop when seen, unless it is found at wave 16-18 or later.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Crown is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Crown is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Crown
sections.text:
The Crown increases the amount of Harvesting you gain at the end of the wave by 8%. The Crown can provide large amounts of harvesting if found early. See the Harvesting page for more details.
Note that the Crown stops functioning after wave 20 when playing Endless Mode as the Harvesting Skill begins to decline.
The Crown is particularly powerful on characters with high amounts of Harvesting, such as Farmer or Entrepreneur.

name: Crystal

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Crystal is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
In addition to the permanent +5% Attack Speed, Crystal also gives you +1% attack speed every second that you're able to not take damage. If you take damage, the bonus is lost and it starts counting again. The bonus is also lost at the end of each wave.
Self inflicted damage from Blood Donation or Sick will reset the bonus.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

notes:
In addition to the permanent +5% Attack Speed, Crystal also gives you +1% attack speed every second that you're able to not take damage. If you take damage, the bonus is lost and it starts counting again. The bonus is also lost at the end of each wave.
Self inflicted damage from Blood Donation or Sick will reset the bonus.
