sections.heading: Scaling
sections.text: Multiple Cute Monkeys are Additive. So 2 Cute Monkeys give 16% chance to trigger for 1 HP. And 5 Monkeys gives 40% and so on.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Cyberball is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Cyberball
sections.text:
Cyberball's damage is a direct effect, does not crit, lifesteal, nor do "normal" attack things. They will not target Trees.
Similarly to the Baby Elephant, the Cyberball's damage dealt is affected by the player's Damage stat -- so a player with 100 Luck and +20% Damage will deal 30 damage on a Cyberball trigger (25 base + 5).

sections.heading: Scaling
sections.text:
If you have more than one, each Cyberball will trigger independently each time you kill an enemy.
So for example if you have 3 Cyberballs and kill an enemy, another enemy is chosen, and then the game rolls a 100 sided die three times. For each successful roll, the target takes damage. So with 3 Cyberballs that deal 10 damage on hit, the target could take 0, 10, 20, or 30 damage.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Price decreased: 35 => 30

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Cyclops Worm is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

name: Dangerous Bunny

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Dangerous Bunny is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Dangerous Bunny
sections.text:
Each Dangerous Bunny that is owned grants 1 additional free reroll in the Shop.
The free rerolls are granted for each shop, but unused free rerolls do not carry over to the next shop (i.e. they can not be saved for later).
The item count is limited to 3 per run, so at most 3 free rerolls per shop can be gained with this item.
The free rerolls always happen before the regular rerolls, and do not increase the reroll cost for subsequent (regular) rerolls.
For example on Wave 7, rerolls cost 10 materials for the first reroll, and +3 for each subsequent reroll.
So normally the price would be 10/13/16 for the first 3 rerolls. If you have 1 Bunny, it becomes 0/10/13. And if you have 2: 0/0/10, 0/0/0 for 3 bunnies.
Also see Shop Rerolling.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
Dangerous Bunny is an Economy Item that will save you a lot of materials over the duration of a run. If bought early in a run, it will net you far more materials than its price.
The item is very strong in endless mode, where it continues to provide its benefit past wave 20.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
When a Dangerous Bunny is purchased, instead of granting a free reroll, it simply reduces the cost of the current reroll to 0, meaning that the price of rerolls that follow increase unlike normal.
The Tooltip for how much the Bunny has saved you is inaccurate. It counts the first reroll price, so if your reroll price was 10, 13, 16 for rerolls (Wave 7 costs), and it reduced it to 0, 10, 13, then it will say that you saved 10 materials. But you actually saved 16 gold in total if you wanted to reroll 3 times.
And if you have more Dangerous Bunnies, it will still only say 10 gold per bunny, even that those 3 rerolls would be at least worth 39 gold (10+13+16) and potentially more, since the 4th and onwards rerolls are cheaper.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Price decreased: 35 => 30

notes:

sections.heading: Item
sections.text:
Dynamite is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.
Dynamite increases the secondary stat Explosive Damage, which increases Damage of weapons with the explosive tag, and the damage of all explosions. This also increases the "non explosion" part of the weapons, such as the projectiles of Shredder or the damage of a Plank melee attack.
Damage and Explosive Damage stacks stacks additively. So if you have a Dynamite and +30% damage, your explosive weapons will deal +45% damage in total.

sections.heading: Esty's Couch
sections.text: Esty's Couch provides a great amount of HP Regeneration, if you have low enough speed. The Couch only accounts for permanent changes to your speed, and doesn't includes the temperary decreases like the one Streamer gets while carrying materials.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Explosive Shells is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Explosive Shells
sections.text:
Explosive Damage stacks additively with normal % Damage, so the net gain is +45% damage with any weapon with the explosive tag, and all explosive effects.
Explosive Shells is a very powerful item if all or most of your damage is done by explosions and/or explosive weapons. However it does come with -15% Damage, so if one or more of your weapons are non-explosive consider if Explosive Shells is worth it.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text:
Explosive Turret is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.
Buying the item spawns an Explosive Turret at the beginning of each wave that attacks enemies within 500 range for 25 + (175% ) damage, attacking every 0.87 seconds.
It is identical to the turret spawned by a Wrench Tier 4.

sections.heading: Scaling
sections.text:
Turrets are unaffected by Primary Stats, such as Damage, Life Steal, Attack Speed, Crit Chance. But is affected by Engineering.
Turrets are affected by Secondary Stats such as piercing and bouncing granted by the items Bandana & Ricochet. 
 Explosive Turret benefits from Explosion Damage and Size granted by Dynamite & Plastic Explosive. 
Turrets can inflict burning with Scared Sausage, and Snake helps Incendiary Turret inflict burn to more targets.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Extra Stomach is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Extra Stomach is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Extra Stomach
sections.text:
The Extra Stomach grants +1 permanent Max HP whenever you eat a Consumable while at full HP, with a maximum of 8 per wave. Note that all consumables are automatically attracted to you and eaten at the end of each wave. So you don't need to collect the consumables during the wave.
The Extra Stomach is more powerful the earlier you pick it up. It can provide a large amount of Max HP over several waves, and is a very powerful item for Endless Mode
You can have more consumables dropped by increasing your Luck stat, or by buying the Garden item.
When Druid picks up a poisoned fruit, it will increase your Max HP before reducing your health.

sections.heading: Update History

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Eyes Surgery is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Effect
sections.text:
Eyes Surgery reduces the time between each tick of burning damage by 20%, or 0.2 second for each Eyes Surgery you have. This means that targets with the burn debuff will take damage every 0.2/0.6 second. This leads to a 25%/67% increase in the damage per second done by the burn effect.
However the total amount of ticks doesn't increase. So if the burn debuff deals 10x3 damage, it will still only deal 30 damage in total, but it will happen faster.
Since multiple burn debuffs doesn't stack, but instead refreshes the duration of the burn, this might not be an issue.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Burning activation bonus increased: 10% => 20%. Limit changed: 3 => 2. Gained +1 Elemental Damage

sections.heading: Fairy
sections.text: As of Version 1.0.1.3 there are 53 unique Tier I Items and 31 unique Tier IV Items . Therefore the maximum possible +HP Regeneration is +53 (all unique Tier I, 0 Tier IV), the minimum possible is -62 (0 Tier I, all Tier IV), and the Fairy is a net negative of -9 HP Regeneration if you have all unique items for both Tier I and Tier IV.

name: Fish Hook

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Fish Hook is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Fish Hook causes Locked items and weapons in the Shop to have +20% chance to be cursed when entering the shop. Multiple Fish hooks stack additively, so 2 regular Fish Hooks means 40% chance of Cursing each wave.
Cursed Fish Hook can have up to 42% chance to curse locked items in the shop. So it is possible to reach 100% chance to curse after locking in the Shop. This is quite useful for Endless Mode.

sections.heading: Pity System
sections.text:
The Fish Hook has a pity system which increases the odds of success whenever you enter a shop and fail to curse any locked items.
When you enter a shop after failing to curse any locked item, you gain a 25% bonus to your curse chance per item you had locked. So if you had 40% chance to curse, you'll get a 10% Pity Bonus per locked item you had. If 1 or more of the locked items got cursed, you get no pity bonus.
The moment Fish Hook manages to curse an item, it removes the pity bonus. So if you have 4 locked items and a large pity bonus, it is likely that the first item it attempts to curse actually gets cursed. In that case, the pity bonus is instantly lost, and the last 3 locked items don't benefit from the locked items. Note it is random which item gets cursed first.
Finding an "organically" Cursed item in the shop doesn't reset the pity bonus. It is only reset if an item is cursed by the Fish Hook effect.
If you have a large pity bonus, consider being slightly more picky about which items you lock.
You can see the current Pity Bonus in the tooltip of a Fish Hook.
The pity bonus is rounded down. So if the calculated bonus would be 9.5, it gets rounded down to 9.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text:
1.1.7.1: Added the Pity system.
1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

notes:
Fish Hook causes Locked items and weapons in the Shop to have +20% chance to be cursed when entering the shop. Multiple Fish hooks stack additively, so 2 regular Fish Hooks means 40% chance of Cursing each wave.
Cursed Fish Hook can have up to 42% chance to curse locked items in the shop. So it is possible to reach 100% chance to curse after locking in the Shop. This is quite useful for Endless Mode.

name: Frozen Heart

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Frozen Heart is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Frozen Heart is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Frozen Heart is a powerful option for elemental builds. It offers a ton of Elemental Damage and some Crit Chance at the cost of slowing down the tick-rate of your Burn effects.
Burning activates 100% slower means that burn will do damage every 2 seconds instead of every second. If you have 20% faster burn rate from an Eyes Surgery, it simply stacks addively, so burn will activate every 1.8 second.
Frozen Heart technically makes your weapons into elemental scaling weapons, which means they will trigger Ice Cube.
Be careful with taking Frozen Heart when playing Chef, as it will make all your weapons count as elemental weapons due to the scaling. Meaning they can't gain the +200% bonus damage from burning enemies.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Garden is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Garden
sections.text:
The Garden item spawns a small garden at the start of each wave for each Garden item you have. Each garden drop consumables as soon as they spawn, and then continuedly spawns fruits throughout the wave with a 15 secound cooldown.
The Pruner weapon also spawns gardens that produce fruits with a 15/14/12/10 second cooldown.
Gardens are Structures, so they benefit from Improved Tools and Clockwork Wasp, reducing the cooldown of fruits spawning and it grants +2 Armor per garden to Streamer.
The healing of the fruits can be increased by Lemonade or Weird Food and can grant Max HP if you have the Extra Stomach item. Or extra harvesting for the Farmer and Explosive Damage with Glutton.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Giant Belt is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Giant Belt is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Giant Belt
sections.text:
Giant Belt deals Health Percent Damage whenever you deal a critical hit to an enemy. It deals 10% of Current Health of regular Enemies, and 1% of Elites or Bosses. This amount can be increased on a Cursed version of Giant Belt.
This means that if you attack an enemy with a critical hit that deals 50 damage, and the enemy has 1000 current HP, the belt will cause the attack to deal 150 damage (10% of 1000 + 50) damage to the target, or 60 damage (1% of 1000 + 50) if the target was an elite or boss.
The added damage cannot be modified. Not by your % Damage, not by Crit Multipliers. Not by Silver Bullet, or Small Fish.

sections.heading: Structures
sections.text: When using the Pile of Books, you can make Structures Crit. This works with Giant Belt.

sections.heading: Endless Scaling
sections.text:
Giant Belt is coded to lose effectiveness when you get to the higher levels of Endless Mode. Starting at wave 39, the belt begins to deal less than 10% and 1% of current health. You can see the percentage of effectiveness for the Belt below:

name: Goldfish

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Goldfish is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
The Goldfish is a single use item that is used during Shopping, similarly to Mirror.
When you reroll the shop while having a goldfish active, the items and weapons on the new reroll will be +1 tier higher than they would've otherwise have been. So if the shop had selected to give you a tier 2 weapon, that becomes tier 3, and it then chooses the specific weapon to give from among tier 3 weapons.
After being used, the Goldfish turns into a Resting Goldfish, which does nothing.
The tooltip can be a bit hard to understand. But the goldfish doesn't affect Locked items. And if you reroll while having locked items, you'll see less than 4 items/weapons that have their tier boosted by the goldfish, wasting some of the potential.
Goldfish can make Tier 4 items and weapons show up in the shop before it would otherwise be possible. Normally Tier 4 can't show up before wave 8+.
Cursed Goldfish upgrades the next reroll by 2-3 tiers. At +3, this guarantees a full shop of Tier 4 items.
If you have multiple Goldfish active, for example after using a Mirror to buy two goldfishes, then only one Goldfish is used per reroll. It still only gives +1 to tiers.
When playing Curious be warned that since the Goldfish turns into another item after being used, it will show the Green icon even if it isn't the first Goldfish you buy. But you'll end up with 2 Used Goldfishes, which will give you -10% Damage.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

name: Greek Fire

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Greek Fire is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Greek Fire is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text: Greek Fire makes your Burn deal bonus Health Percent Damage to enemies each second.

sections.heading: Works with Structures
sections.text: You can also trigger Greek Fire with structures. Incendiary Turret's blue fire triggers it. And if you have Scared Sausage, your structures can inflict burn on hit, which triggers greek fire.

sections.heading: Cannot be modified
sections.text:
You cannot increase the damage done by HP%DMG. It isn't modified by anything.
The Damage dealt by HP%DMG isn't increased by % Damage, or multiplied by Critical Hits. It isn't increased by Silver Bullet, or Small Fish.

sections.heading: Cursed
sections.text: When Cursed, the HP%DMG damage effect is multiplied by the Curse boost. So 10%/1% (Regular/Elite) damage per hit can be increased all the way to 21%/2.1%. Note that the 10 to 1 ratio between damage to regular enemies and damage to elite/bosses is kept.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

notes: Greek Fire makes your Burn deal bonus Health Percent Damage to enemies each second.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Handcuffs is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Handcuffs is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Handcuffs
sections.text:
Handcuffs provide a large amount of damage for a small cost. But it comes with the downside of locking your Max HP when you buy it. You can still increase or decrease your Max HP Stat, but when you enter the game, your health bar can't be higher than your Max Health cap. For example, if you buy handcuffs while you have 50 Max HP, your Max HP can never exceed 50. However, you can still buy Max HP-reducing items and have less than 50 Max HP. The lock doesn't prevent you from getting lower.
Even if you buy Handcuffs, HP-Scaling Items and Weapons like Alien Eyes, Potato Thrower, and Chopper can still scale beyond the Max HP cap because they scale with the stat itself. The cap only affects your HP Bar.
Handcuffs can be a powerful pickup late in the game if you've already gotten the Max HP you wanted for the wave 20 boss fight.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Hunting Trophy is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Hunting Trophy
sections.text:
Hunting Trophy gives you a chance to gain Materials when you kill an enemy by a critical hit. Similar to the Thief Dagger.
Unlike the Materials gained by Harvesting, Hunting Trophies don't give Experience. It only gives you the Currency Part of Materials.

sections.heading: Stacking
sections.text:
The stacking of Hunting Trophy was changed with the Patch 1.1.0.0 to being additively. That means you have 33% with 1 Hunting trophy, 66% with 2, and 99% with 3 Hunting Trophies to get 1 gold on a kill. This amounts to the same average as before the change. However with Cursed versions of Hunting Trophies, you can get above 100% chance. But you're now capped at 100% chance to get 1 gold.
For that reason, it might not be needed to try to get multiple cursed Hunting Trophies when playing Endless Mode, since there's no benefit to going above 100%.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Stacking method changed from independently to additive. Meaning that Hunting Trophies now just add their % to give Materials on Kill together for a max of 100%.

name: Improved Tools

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Improved Tools is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Improved Tools is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Improved Tools
sections.text:
Improved Tools gives 10% Attack Speed, and increases the attack speed and/or spawning rate of all Engineering Structures by half of your Attack Speed Stat. See the Structures page for more details on structures and their default attack speed.
This applies to Landmines, which will spawn faster, and Garden which will generate Consumables faster.
So if you have +100% Attack speed Stat, Improved Tools will make Turrets attack 50% faster, and Gardens spawn Consumables 50% faster.
The Improved Tools affect all structures with the exception of Wandering Bot, which doesn't attack or spawn anything. Improved tools affect structures no matter their source, so it doesn't matter if a Turret is spawned by the item Turret, the Wrench weapon, or the Pocket Factory item.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
Improved Tools tend to be very powerful on Engineering builds, provided you have the attack speed to back it up. Outside of this item, Structures don't benefit from Attack speed, so you might dump the attack speed stat and let it go into the negative values.
It is furthermore very strong on builds that heavily rely on Garden, such as Pruner builds. These are common builds for Farmer and Glutton.
When planning an Engineering build, it can be a good strategy to anticipate picking up this item (especially on Due to Item Tags, the Character(s) Builder, Cyborg, Dwarf, Engineer, Technomage have a higher chance to find these items in the Shop. that are tagged for Engineering, so this item is more likely to drop). In this case, you can build Attack Speed early in order to help this item's effect later on. But be warned that Improved Tools is far from guaranteed to drop during a run.
Finally, Improved Tools can be worth it on regular weapon builds that need the attack speed and have a few structures such as a Medical Turret or a Garden. Especially if you already have a good amount of attack speed. For example on One Armed, the 200% attack speed the character starts with, means that Improved Tools will double the attack speed of all structures.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text: The tooltip of items like Garden shows the drop rate rounded to the nearest second. The internal value used in the game includes decimals however. This is the case for all structures.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Gained the Attack Speed Tag

notes: The tooltip of items like Garden shows the drop rate rounded to the nearest second. The internal value used in the game includes decimals however. This is the case for all structures.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text:
Incendiary Turret is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.
Buying the item spawns an Incendiary Turret at the beginning of each wave that attacks enemies within 300 range for 1 damage, piercing enemies and inflicts burn to targets dealing 5 + (33% ) damage every second for 8 seconds. Attacks every 0.28s.
It is identical to the turret spawned by a Wrench Tier 2.

sections.heading: Scaling
sections.text:
Turrets being Structures are unaffected by Primary Stats, such as Damage, Life Steal, Attack Speed, Crit Chance. But are affected by Engineering.
Turrets are affected by Secondary Stats such as piercing and bouncing granted by the items Bandana & Ricochet. The Incendiary Turret get +1 Burn damage from Scared Sausage, and Snake helps inflict burn to more targets.
Incendiary Turret will not apply Ice Cube's effect, as it does not scale with Elemental Damage.

name: Jelly

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Jelly is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Jelly gives you Max HP. Considering that it has the same price as Chopper, once you have 3+ different weapons, Jelly becomes a better item than the Cake. Generally speaking Jelly becomes good with 3+ weapons.
Jelly is a great item for Characters such as Curious, Gladiator, or Vagabond that are pushed towards having different weapons.
Jelly can insane amounts of Max HP on characters that can have more than 6 weapons: Baby and Multitasker
The Max HP granted by Jelly is consider permanent and can be spent in the Shop by Demon and Chunky gains bonus % Damage from it. It also scales weapons such as Chopper that scale with Max HP.

sections.heading: Update History

name: Knot

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Knot is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Knot is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Knot prevents you from selling and combining weapons. It doesn't prevent you from buying weapons. So if you see it in the Shop, you can combine your weapons before buying it, leaving you with empty weapon slots which you can fill in later shops.
Arms Dealer can make good use of Knot, since they lose all their weapons when entering the shop. So they rely less on conbining weapons, and don't need the ability to sell weapons.
Knot gives a ton of stats for its price, due to the heavy downside. It is similar to Handcuffs, Shackles, and Ball and Chain in that it offers a ton of stats, but locks on of your stats. These items are generally great to find just before the run ends, since the lock downside isn't going to affect you.
If you have Anvil and Knot, the Anvil will give you Armor since it can't upgrade your Weapons

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

sections.text: Landmines is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Landmines
sections.text:
Buying the item spawns 1 Landmine at the beginning of each wave and every 12 seconds during the wave. The mine explodes when enemies step off the mine dealing 10 + (100% ) damage to enemies in an area.
Screwdriver spawns the same Landmines, but with different cooldown depending on the tier of the Screwdriver.
The explosion can inflict burn with the Scared Sausage item.

sections.heading: Scaling
sections.text:
Landmines are unaffected by Primary Stats, such as Damage, Life Steal, Attack Speed, Crit Chance. But is affected by Engineering.
Landmines are affected by Secondary Stats such as Explosion Damage and Size granted by Dynamite & Plastic Explosive.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Crit Multiplier increased from 1.5x to 2x. (Landmines couldn't crit before this patch, and this change was made as Pile of Books was added to the game)

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Every 3 Seconds, a yellow glowing area pulses around you, inflicting knockback on every enemy within about 150 range of you. The knockback distance depends on your knockback stat.
When Cursed the cooldown of the Knockback pulse is also reduced, such that it happens every 2/1 second depending on the strength of the curse.

sections.text:

Since Luck is a useful stat for just about every build, the Lucky Coin can be a powerful pickup for any build with high amounts of Crit Chance.

sections.heading: Medical Turret
sections.text:
Buying the item spawns a Turret at the beginning of each wave that shoots healing projectiles towards you while you are within 400 range for 3 + (5% ) healing every 2.2 seconds.
Note that the engineering scaling rounds down. So it takes 20 or more engineering for the turret to heal 4 HP. While it only takes -1 or more engineering for it to only heal 2 HP per shot. So think twice about buying Ritual if you have a medical Turret on a non engineering build.
The Medical Turret is a Structure and is unaffected by Primary Stats, such as Damage, Life Steal, Attack Speed, Crit Chance. But is affected by Engineering.

name: Metal Detector

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Metal Detector is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Metal detector gives an additive 5% per item chance for any Materials you pick up to have double the value. You gain both double the Money and double Experience.
Buccaneer's double materials ability gives 4x materials when Metal Detector triggers.
Double value materials from bagged materials dropped at the beginning of a wave gives 4x the value when metal detector triggers. This means you gain same amount of materials on average if you let your materials get bagged or not. See the Materials page for details on bagged materials.
It also doubles any other materials that for any reason have their value increased. For example if more than 50 materials are on the ground. Again, see the materials page for details.
It is a very powerful item for Endless Mode, where the ability to gain additional materials is highly valued.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Padding is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Padding
sections.text:
Paddings gives +3 to your Max HP and a further increase depending on your current carried materials. You gain +1 Max HP per 80 materials you have. This is updated dynamically, meaning that your Max HP will increase during a wave as you pick up more materials, but this Max HP is also lost when you spend your materials in the Shop.
The Temporary Materials based Max HP from Padding doesn't work with Chunky's Damage increase from max HP. However, it does work with the Max HP scaling of items/weapons such as Chopper, Potato Thrower, and Alien Eyes.
When playing Demon, the Max HP granted from Padding can't be spent in the shop.
Padding can be powerful when combined with strategies of saving up large amounts of materials, such as with Piggy Bank or with the Character Saver.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: limited 3 => Unique. Now has Max HP tag instead of Saving. 1 HP per 100 => 1 HP per 80 materials. -5% Speed downside removed.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Peaceful Bee is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Peacock is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Peacock
sections.text:
Peacock grants a consistant +25% exp gain, but also a temporary +100% exp gain for the next wave. However, enemies for the next wave also have +50% damage during that wave.
Timing your purchase of the Peacock is key. Buying the peacock before a wave that grants a large amount of experience, such as wave 9 or a horde wave, can give you a very large boost.
However, be careful about buying the peacock before a dangerous wave, such as wave 14 where the large amount of projectiles can be especially dangerous, or Elite waves.
You can lock the Peacock in the shop for a round or more to make the timing better, but remember that locking an item means seeing less items in the shop, so don't lock it for several rounds.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Piggy Bank is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Piggy Bank is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Piggy Bank
sections.text:
The Piggy Bank give you 20% of your Materials when you exit the shop and start a new wave.
For example, if you leave the shop with 100 materials, you will start the wave with 120.
Unlike the Materials gained by Harvesting, Piggy Bank doesn't give Experience. It only gives you the Currency Part of Materials.
While playing in Endless Mode, Piggy Bank stops functioning after wave 20.
The item tooltip shows the total amount of materials generated by the item, but does not take into account the purchase cost of the item itself, if any.

sections.heading: Strategy
sections.text:
Piggy Bank is an Economic Item that can earn you a ton of Materials if you're ahead and can afford to save up Materials between rounds. Like other Economic items, it is more powerful when bought early, and of little value when bought in the late game.
The amount of materials generated by the Piggy Bank is substantial, due to the high growth rate of 20%, and because the process is multiplicative (compounding). Any amount held back will more than double over four waves, and will have multiplied by ~6.2 over ten waves.
So when you have the Piggy Bank, you need to carefully evaluate how many materials you want to spend when you're shopping. It might not be worth it reroll as much. And some economic items might not be worth their cost, as the materials could be saved and multiply over multiple waves.
If you can't save up materials between rounds, it might be better to skip the Piggy Bank, since it will barely recoup its purchasing price.
The Piggy Bank has strong synergy with characters such as Demon or Streamer that naturally tend to have materials saved up between waves. The Character Saver starts the game with Piggy Bank and has their entire gameplay built around the item.

name: Pile Of Books

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Pile of Books is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Pile of Books is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Pile of Books makes your Crit Chance stat apply to Structures including Landmines. Structures have a base 0% Crit Chance compared to the standard 3% that most Weapons have.
Pile of Books can be hard to make work in regular mode, since some engineering items reduce Crit Chance: Compass and Pencil . But is amazing for Endless Mode, where it lets you trigger Hunting Trophy.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

sections.heading: Plastic Explosion

Plastic Explosion increases the size of any explosion you cause by 25% per Plastic Explosive you have. It is multiplicative with the base explosion radius of an explosion.
See the Explosion page for a list of Explosion Sizes.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Pocket Factory is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Pocket Factory is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Pocket Factory
sections.text:
When you have Pocket Factory, a Turret will spawn every time you kill a Tree. The turret spawns next to where the tree was. You can increase the amount of trees that spawn by buying more Tree items. Over the duration of a wave, this can result in quite the number of turrets.
Pocket Factory counts as having 1 Structure for effects such as Armor for Streamer or negative Engineering for Lighthouse

sections.heading: Update History

sections.heading: Effect
sections.text: Power Generator increases your % Damage depending on your Permanent speed stat. Temporary increases or decreases, such as those granted by the Streamer or Golem's abilities, do not impact the number used for Power Generator. Once you have +25% speed or more, Power Generator becomes one of the most efficient ways to increase your % Damage.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Pumpkin is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Effect
sections.text:
Some Ranged Weapons have Piercing, allowing their projectiles to pierce through, and hit more than one target. However, weapons have a default 50% damage falloff on the projectile for each time it pierces. Each Pumpkin you have reduces this falloff by 15%. So if you have a pumpkin, only 35% damage is lost on each pierce.
Pumpkins can be stacked, removing 15% each. If you have 4 pumpkins, for a total of 60% reduced damage, the 50% damage falloff is completely removed, and the attacks will deal full damage on each piercing/hit.
Some some weapons have innate piercing, and come with a different damage falloff. Double Barrel Shotgun only loses 30% Damage per Pierce, so it only needs 2 Pumpkins for full damage. Shredder has a default damage falloff of 0%, meaning it won't need any Pumpkins
The Damage falloff modification done by Pumpkin is affected by the All Damage Modification changes that characters such as Entrepreneur, who only gains 7.5% Less Damage falloff from a pumpkin (Due to 50% less damage modifications), or One Armed, who gains a whole 30% Damage falloff per Pumpkin. (+100% damage modifications).
See Piercing for more info and examples.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Recycling Machine is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Recycling Machine is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Recycling Machine
sections.text:
You can recycle your Weapons and Items found in Crates to get a percentage of their value back, at the cost of losing the item or weapon.
The Default is to get 25% back when recycling. Recycling Machine can increase this value by 35% for a total of 60% back.
This stacks with the +25% from Entrepreneur for a total of 85%. Rewards for recycling are based upon the current price of the item, so it increases with inflation, but decreases if you have coupons, since it scales with your shop price secondary stat.

name: Regeneration Potion

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Regeneration Potion is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Regeneration Potion is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
The Regeneration Potion gives you a special ability to double your HP Regeneration when you are below half of your Max HP.
The Regeneration Potion makes your HP Regeneration heal +2 HP per tick of healing, rather than doubling the rate of ticks.
The Regeneration Potion is a very powerful item on builds that relies on HP Regeneration for healing, commonly seen on characters such as Bull, Doctor, Lich, or Masochist

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Retromation's Hoodie is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Retromation's Hoodie is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Retromation's Hoodie
sections.text:
Retromation's Hoodie is not affected by the dodge cap and continues to provide Attack Speed when going above the default 60% dodge cap. For example, 75% Dodge will give 150 Attack Speed.
Once you've picked up Retromation's Hoodie, Dodge becomes one of, if not the most important stat you can find until you hit the Dodge Cap.
Even after you hit the Dodge Cap, it continues to be one of the most efficient sources of Attack Speed. For example, a Tier 2 Upgrade gives 10% attack speed. But if you have Retromation's Hoodie, you can take a Tier 2 6% dodge upgrade and gain +12% attack speed.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Price increased: 100 => 110

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Ricochet is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Ricochet
sections.text:
The Ricochet increases the number of bounces for projectiles by 1. This includes ranged weapons, Turrets, and projectiles spawned by weapons such as Cacti Club.
It is a very useful item to help single target ranged weapons keep up with large hordes of enemies.
Projectiles have a default damage falloff of 50% when bouncing, unless the weapon have bouncing by default such as the Slingshot or the projectiles spawned by Lightning Shiv.
So the extra hit granted by Ricochet will only deal 50% damage on most weapons. Note that Ricochet should be avoided on weapons that relies on piercing, such as Crossbow or Shredder, as it will halve the damage of all the following piercing shots.
Projectiles with both bounce and pierce will only pierce after they're done bouncing.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text:
Rip and Tear is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.
Rip and Tear cannot crit, and it does not trigger life steal. Enemies killed by Rip and Tear can also cause Rip and Tear to trigger on their deaths. Be mindful that picking up Rip and Tear can severely reduce the amount of healing you get from life steal in general, if the explosions are killing enemies that would otherwise have been hit by your weapons.

sections.heading: Scaling
sections.text: Both the damage and the chance to cause an explosion increases when you gain additional Rip and Tear items. So the chance for an explosion is 20%/40%/60%/80%/100% as you get up to 5 of the item. And the damage also scales up to 50 + 250% of your Melee damage when you have 5 Rip and Tear items.

sections.heading: Explosion
sections.text: Rip and Tear causes Explosions. The size of the explosion can be increased with Plastic Explosive. And the damage can be increased with Dynamite.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Downside changed: -12 Harvesting => -5% Crit Chance

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Scared Sausage is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Scared Sausage
sections.text:
Scared Sausage gives any weapon hit, any projectile hit, and any hit by an Engineering structure a 25% chance to inflict burn.
The burn effect from Scared Sausage does 1 + (100%) damage each second, for up to 3 seconds.
Each additional Scared Sausage increases the chance to inflict burn by another 25%. It is possible to reach a 100% chance by holding 4 Scared Sausages (which is the count limit for this item).
Scared Sausage passively adds 1 to the burn damage of any weapons that inflict burn (e.g Flamethrower), and to other Scared Sausages (if multiple are held). Since Scared Sausage costs more than 1 Elemental Damage, it is not economical to buy the item only to increase the burn damage of weapons.

sections.heading: Effects that can inflict burn
sections.text:
Spawned projectiles from Alien Eyes, Baby with a Beard, Lightning Shiv, Cacti Club and other weapons can inflict burn. (This was not the case, but was changed in patch 1.0.1.3.)
The Explosion from Bull or Landmines can inflict burn, as do explosions from weapons such as Rocket Launcher.
All Engineering structures can inflict burn, via direct hit as well as explosion hit ( Turret, Laser Turret, Explosive Turret, and Tyler).

sections.heading: Effects that can not inflict burn
sections.text:
Baby Elephant, Cyberball, or Riposte can not inflict burn.
The Explosion from items such as Spicy Sauce or Rip and Tear can not inflict burn.

sections.heading: Incendiary Turrets
sections.text:
Incendiary Turret inflicts a separate type of burn ("blue burn"); they can not inflict the burn from Scared Sausage.
The damage of the turret's "blue burn" is increased by 1 for each Scared Sausage.
Snakes do spread the blue fire of the turret.

name: Seashell

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Seashell is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Seashell is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Seashell only affects weapons that shoot projectiles. Ranged Weapons.
It does not work with Structures, such as Turret. It doesn't work with projectiles spawned by items such as Alien Eyes or Baby with a Beard. It does not work with Lightning Shiv or Cacti Club.
Builder's Builder's Turret is able to benefit from Seashell.
The additional projectiles are additive with your weapon's projectiles. That means a Tier 1-3 Double Barrel Shotgun will fire 7 projectiles when Seashell triggers.

sections.text:
Sifd's Relic automatically collects any Materials on the map. This is quite useful for abilities that trigger upon material pickup such as Baby Elephant or Cute Monkey
When Cursed, Sifd's gains +10% Dodge, which is then affected by the curse modifier, giving you more than 10% Dodge.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: The Sifd's Relic is a reference to the Youtuber Sifd, who made the Sifd's Mod for Brotato that was very popular back in the day.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Silver Bullet is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Silver Bullet
sections.text:
Silver Bullet grants 25% Bonus damage against Elites and Bosses Secondary Stat, multiplying any damage you deal to Elites or Bosses.
The damage bonus is multiplicative, meaning that if you have an attack that deals 100 damage, and you have 100% Damage, bringing the attack to 200. Then Silver Bullet will multiply that by another 1.25, bringing it to a total of 250 damage. If you had 4 Silver Bullets, the attack would deal 400 damage.
Silver Bullet is additive with Jack's +125% against Bosses and Elites. It is the same effect.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Snake is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Effect
sections.text:
Snake makes any burn you inflict be able to spread its debuff to any nearby enemy that isn't already burning. Each snake increases the amount of times the burning effect can spread by +1.
The spread can happen at any point during the burning duration. Fire spread by snakes can also spread to more enemies, but it uses same counter, so if you have 3 snakes, it can still only spread a total of 3 times, but any of the targets put on fire can spread it.
Snakes also makes Incendiary Turrets spread their fire to more targets.

name: Snowball

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Snowball is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Snowball is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Snowball gives you additional Elemental Damage whenever you aquire an item that gives elemental damage. It is not retroactive, so you don't get any bonus elemental damage for items you already have.
The bonus is only for items. You do not gain any bonuses for taking Upgrades with elemental damage or Weapons with the Elemental tag.
Finally, the elemental damage gained by Technomage buying structures does not count.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Spicy Sauce is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Quadratic Scaling
sections.text:
Both the damage and the chance to cause an Explosion increases when you gain additional Spicy Sauce Items. So the chance for an explosion is 50%/100% as you get up to 2 of the item. The damage is 100%/200% of your Max HP as you have 1-2 Spicy Sauces.
Because of this quadratic scaling, don't stay at 1 Spicy Sauce! You'll want to pick up 2 if possible, instead of just having 1.
The Tooltip will only display the damage of 100% of your Max HP. So if you have 2 Spicy Sauces, the actual damage of the explosion is 200% of that.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text:
1.1.0.0: Limit changed: 4 => 3. Chance to explode changed: 25% => 33%
1.1.9.0: Limit changed: 3 => 2. Chance to explode changed: 33% => 50%

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Starfish is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Starfish increases the amount of Materials dropped, so it has synergy with Material pickup based effects like Baby Elephant or Cute Monkey. It is also great with Buccaneer, since the value of materials is doubled, meaning you're basically gaining +40% materials.
Since it only affects materials from enemies, it doesn't affect Trees, which are neutral, and Brick.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Stone Skin is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Stone Skin is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Stone Skin only works with permanent armor. So Armor gained by Streamer's ability does not count. Similarly, armor gained by {{{1}}} does not exist as an icon doesn't affect your Max HP either.
Reversely, Romantic's negative armor gained by Curse isn't counted either, so Romantic can actually make quite decent use of Stone Skin.

notes:
Stone Skin only works with permanent armor. So Armor gained by Streamer's ability does not count. Similarly, armor gained by {{{1}}} does not exist as an icon doesn't affect your Max HP either.
Reversely, Romantic's negative armor gained by Curse isn't counted either, so Romantic can actually make quite decent use of Stone Skin.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Sunken Bell is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Sunken Bell is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Sunken Bell causes a huge explosion the first time you fall below 40% on each wave. The explosion is large and
Certain characters gain a ton of flat damage stats and can deal a ton of damage with Sunken Bell: Apprentice, Knight, or Dwarf
Explosion based Characters can also improve Sunken Bell a lot, but might not need the additional explosion: Artificer, Bull, Glutton, or Ogre
Sunken Bell's Explosion benefits greatly from explosion enhancing items like: Dynamite, Plastic Explosive, or Explosive Shells.

sections.heading: Update History

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Tardigrade is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Tardigrade
sections.text:
the Tardigrade provides a shield that protects you against 1 instance of Damage. While you have 1 or more Tardigrades active, protecting you, your Max HP bar will be shown as Purple. You can have more than one Tardigrade, which in turn will protect you from that many instances of Damage.
The Tardigrade is most useful on builds that deal damage at range with a low amount of defenses, often called "Glass Cannon" builds. These builds focus on dealing tons of damage at long range, while ignoring the need for defensive stats. Common Glass cannon builds include Characters such as Hunter, Ranger, and Apprentice.
Self-Damage items such as Blood Donation and Bloody Hand will break the Tardigrade protection (1 per second). So try to avoid picking those up if you already have Tardigrade. Similarly, Sick isn't the best friend of the Tardigrade.
In Endless Mode Tardigrades can become a very powerful tool for runs that attempt to go to very high wave numbers, where it can be your main plan for surviving.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Torture is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Torture is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Torture
sections.text:
When player has torture, they cannot heal in any other way. All other Healing methods such as HP Regeneration, Life Steal, and Consumables cease to function. Torture can be a really powerful item if you have not yet invested heavily in other forms of healing.
Torture is on the surface awful in Endless Mode, where you can heal way more than 5 HP per second. But it also has synergy with Sad Tomato to put you at half 50%, where you'll attract Consumables to deal damage with Spicy Sauce, and torture will make sure you never heal to 100%.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Price decreased: 110 => 100. Healing per second increased: 4 => 5

name: Treasure Map

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Treasure Map is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Multiple Treasure maps stack additively. So you'll have 20%/40%/60%/80%/100% chance to find an additional item in a Loot Crate.
The chance to find additional items is per Loot Crate. So if you find 3 Crates, you can find up to 3 additional items for a total of 6.
Going above 100% Chance by Cursing Treasure maps has no benefit.
You're finding additional Items, not crates, so you don't get additional gold from Bag, if you have those.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Added to the game.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Triangle of Power is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Effect
sections.text:
The Triangle of Power grants you a large amount of Damage for its cost, however it comes with a catch. While you have Triangle of Power, you lose 2% Damage each time you are hit.
The damage resets at the end of a wave, but the item can end up reducing your damage by a lot if you take damage multiple times over the duration of a wave. Self-damage such as Blood Donation or the Character Sick also triggers the effect, and the Triangle of Power should be avoided in those cases.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Removed the "Armor" tag

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Turret is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Turret
sections.text:
Buying the item spawns a Turret at the beginning of each wave that attacks enemies within 300 range for 10 + (80% ) damage, attacking every 0.73 seconds.
It is identical to the turret spawned by a Wrench Tier 1.

sections.heading: Scaling
sections.text:
Turrets being Structures are unaffected by Primary Stats, such as Damage, Life Steal, Attack Speed, Crit Chance. But are affected by Engineering.
Turrets are affected by Secondary Stats such as piercing and bouncing granted by the items Bandana & Ricochet. Turrets can inflict burning with Scared Sausage, and Snake helps inflict burn to more targets.

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Tyler is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Tyler
sections.text: Buying the item spawns a Tyler at the beginning of each wave that shoots lightning in all directions when enemies come within 200 range for 10 (+75% +75%) damage every 2.2 seconds. The lightning pierces up to 2 times.

sections.heading: Scaling
sections.text:
Turrets being Structures are unaffected by Primary Stats, such as Damage, Life Steal, Attack Speed, Crit Chance. But are affected by Engineering.
Turrets are affected by Secondary Stats such as piercing and bouncing granted by the items Bandana & Ricochet. Turrets can inflict burning with Scared Sausage, and Snake helps inflict burn to more targets.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text:
Tyler's design is based on the streamer Olexa, who often plays Brotato. Tyler also appears as a pet in Space Gladiators.
In Freemasonry, a Tyler is a person who guards the door of the Masonic Lodge.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text:
1.1.0.0: Scaling changed: (+100% ) => (+75% +75%).
1.1.0.0: Crit Multiplier increased from 1.5x to 2x. (Tyler couldn't crit before this patch, and this change was made as Pile of Books was added to the game)

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Ugly Tooth is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Unique
sections.text: Ugly Tooth is a unique item. Only 1 of each unique item can be equipped, and it will then no longer be offered in the Shop, and no longer drop from loot crates.

sections.heading: Ugly Tooth
sections.text:
Ugly Tooth slows down enemies when you hit them. The slow stacks up to 4 times for a total of a 20% slow.
The slowdown works on Elites and Bosses, and can make a large difference for those fights.
Ugly Tooth's slow will not be applied via Burning Damage (including Incendiary Turret's Burn) nor via random-target effects like Cyberball.
Structures (such as Turret or Landmines), however, will apply the slow when they hit a target (which does include the non-burn part of an Incendiary Turret's hit). Spawned Projectiles (e.g. Alien Eyes) will also apply the effect.

sections.heading: Update History
sections.text: 1.1.0.0: Slowing nerfed from (3x 10% = 30%) slow to (4x 5% = 20%)

name: Wandering Bot

sections.heading: Item
sections.text: Wandering Bot is an in-game item that can be bought from a Shop or can be found in a Crate. Most Items modify the Stats of the player, and some of them provide additional effects.

sections.heading: Notes
sections.text:
Wandering Bot spawns a small robot that orbits around your character. It slows down any enemies that come close to it, similarly to Taser. Wandering Bot can slow any enemy including Bosses and Elites.
Wandering Bot counts as a Structure and gives +2 Armor/Elemental Damage with Streamer or Technomage.
When Cursed, Wandering bot gains +5% Speed, which is then affected by the curse modifier, giving you more than 5% speed.

sections.text: 1.1.4.0: Can now nolonger be found on Sick. This put an end to one of the beloved community memes, as many players have made the mistake of buying Weird Ghost on Sick at some point of their Brotato Career.

sections.heading: Elites
sections.text: Elites are special enemies that only appear on certain waves. If any elites will spawn in a run the the player will be notified in the Shop. No elites will spawn in dangers 0, 1 or 2 but 1 wave can have an elite enemy on dangers 2 and 3 and a maximum of 3 waves can have a elite on it on dangers 4 and 5. If you kill an elite then it will drop a red crate with a guaranteed drop of a legendary item in side of it.

sections.heading: Mutation 0
sections.text:
(initial phase)
Charges to the player every 2 seconds, shooting projectiles to its left and right during the charge.

sections.heading: Mutation 1
sections.text:
(60% hp or or After 25s)
Charges to the player every 1.3 seconds for shorter distance, shooting projectiles to its left and right during the charge, at the start of its charge fires extra 2 projectiles.

If you kill an elite then it will drop a red crate with a guaranteed drop of a legendary item in side of it.

sections.heading: Bosses
sections.text: Bosses are special enemies that only appear on wave 20. Only one boss will spawn in dangers 0-4 but both will spawn in danger 5 although they have 25% less health. These are the most powerful type of enemy with 29900 health and the player will win the run when all bosses on the wave have been killed or the wave that the boss is on ends due to time. Just like elites, they can't be buffed by the buffer enemy.
